app.name: Gamma
app.bestFor: The fastest path to a polished first draft — a finished, web-style deck in under 90 seconds with strong visual consistency.

app.name: Plus AI
app.bestFor: Professional users working inside Google Slides and PowerPoint — generate, rewrite and reformat slides natively.

app.name: Canva
app.bestFor: Design polish with minimal effort — AI-assisted layouts and templates for visually clean, professional slides.

app.name: Microsoft Copilot for PowerPoint
app.bestFor: Teams living in PowerPoint who want AI generation, redesign suggestions and Agent Mode inside the world's most-used deck tool.

app.name: SlidesAI
app.bestFor: Turning raw text or a script into a full deck — outlines, visuals and speaker notes — inside Google Slides.

app.name: Prezent.ai
app.bestFor: Enterprise teams producing on-brand investor decks and business reviews without leaning on designers.

skill.name: reveal.js
skill.bestFor: The de-facto standard open-source HTML presentation framework (~72k stars) — Markdown authoring, PDF export, speaker notes, LaTeX and auto-animate. The most-adopted tool in the entire category.

skill.name: Slidev
skill.bestFor: The modern developer favorite (~47.8k stars): Markdown-driven decks powered by Vite/Vue 3 with live coding, Mermaid, KaTeX and PPTX/PDF/PNG export. Also ships its own official Agent Skill (see below).

skill.name: impress.js
skill.bestFor: Prezi-style CSS3 3D and zoom transitions with full HTML/CSS/JS control (~38.2k stars). Very high adoption, though less actively maintained than reveal.js and Slidev.

skill.name: pptx (Anthropic official skill)
skill.bestFor: Anthropic's first-party SKILL.md for building, reading and editing real .pptx/.potx files — HTML-to-PPTX generation plus raw OOXML editing. The strongest skill in the category and the one that produces genuinely editable PowerPoint; lives in the 163.9k-star anthropics/skills repo.

skill.name: remark
skill.bestFor: A simple in-browser, Markdown-driven slideshow (~13k stars) for people comfortable with HTML/CSS. A long-standing lightweight option.

skill.name: Spectacle
skill.bestFor: A React/JSX-based presentation library (~10.1k stars) from Formidable Labs, strong for live code demos and the React ecosystem; actively maintained.

skill.name: python-pptx
skill.bestFor: The foundational Python library for programmatically generating and reading .pptx (~3.5k stars). Star count is modest but real-world adoption is enormous — it is the engine the Anthropic pptx skill and most PPTX tooling depend on, so it ranks above higher-starred JS tools on adoption depth.

skill.name: Marp (marp-cli)
skill.bestFor: A mature Markdown-to-slides ecosystem (~3.7k stars for the CLI, plus a popular VS Code extension) exporting HTML/PDF/PPTX. Lower star count than the leaders but a well-adopted Markdown-native toolchain.

skill.name: Slidev official skill
skill.bestFor: A first-party Agent Skill (SKILL.md) shipped inside the Slidev repo that teaches an agent Slidev syntax, animations, diagrams, themes and export. Official skill from a 47.8k-star project; adoption of the skill itself is still new, hence medium.

skill.name: academic-pptx-skill (Gabberflast)
skill.bestFor: A community SKILL.md (~716 stars) that layers academic-deck discipline (action titles, situation-complication-resolution, citation standards) on top of Anthropic's pptx skill. Well-scoped complement rather than a standalone generator.

notes: Skill/Repo track rebuilt and re-ranked by real quality/reputation/adoption (verified GitHub stars, official status) rather than by form factor — 'kind' is a tag only. It now mixes notable open-source repos and genuine SKILL.md Agent Skills. reveal.js leads (~72k stars) as the unambiguous open-source presentation leader, with Slidev and impress.js close behind. Anthropic's official pptx skill ranks 4th and is the top skill: unlike the JS frameworks it produces genuinely editable .pptx files, built on python-pptx (7th). Slidev also ships its own official skill. All stars verified via the GitHub API. No MCP servers; the prior MCP entries (Office-PowerPoint-MCP-Server, SlideSpeak/SlidesGPT MCP) were removed as out of scope.
